question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNormalizedArrayValue()] Can an unprivileged external user send ?timestamp=1234567890.000000001&timestamp=lt:1234567890.000000002 to /api/v1/tokens/:id (matching the documented historical-custom-fees.json example), exploiting NON_SORTED_PARAMS order preservation to get a 200 response, while the reversed order returns a 404, effectively making token transaction history appear or disappear?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: normalizeRequestQueryParams()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that normalizeRequestQueryParams() processes parameters in OpenAPI spec definition order (not user-supplied order), causing the normalized URL to always reorder parameters to a canonical form, which when used as a cache key could cause two semantically different requests to share the same cache entry and serve wrong transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNormalizedArrayValue()] Can an unprivileged external user send ?account.id=eq:1001&account.id=ne:3 to /api/v1/accounts, exploiting the lexicographic sort of non-NON_SORTED_PARAMS (eq:1001 < ne:3 lexicographically), to verify whether the sort produces a different filter order than intended and reorganizes which accounts/transactions are returned?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNormalizedArrayValue()] Can an unprivileged external user send ?account.id=ne:3&account.id=eq:1001 to /api/v1/accounts, exploiting the lexicographic sort of non-NON_SORTED_PARAMS, to verify whether the sort reorders these to eq:1001&ne:3 and whether this reordering changes the SQL query behavior and reorganizes the visible account transaction history?
